title: Chesneya nubigena
taxonomy: Chesneya nubigena is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the genus Chesneya, which is a group of plants native to the Himalayan region. Chesneya nubigena is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae, one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. The species is typically found in high-altitude areas of the Himalayas, where it thrives in rocky and mountainous terrain. Its classification reflects its unique adaptations to the harsh, alpine environment in which it grows.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
